sourceMovie.title: The Boxtrolls
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Boxtrolls works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lego Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Boxtrolls clicked for you, The Lego Movie (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Boxtrolls?
faq.answer: Try The Lego Movie, The Willoughbys, Chickenhare and the Hamster of Darkness—each shares DNA with The Boxtrolls while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Boxtrolls?
faq.answer: The Boxtrolls (2014) is best described as Animation, Comedy, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Boxtrolls worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
